title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Modular Congruence
summary: An implementation team manipulates big integers without normalizing signs, confuses modular inverse with division, and mistakes a worked toy example for secure key generation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the modular congruence increment in bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Perform successful-login rehash, legacy migration, pepper custody and rotation, dummy verification, rate limits, and account recovery without weakening enumeration defenses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Modular Congruence · Theory Crypto Go Modular Congruence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 4386 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
